compiled_state.title: As a developer, I can generate a stack-aware Semgrep security configuration for my project
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: A new generate-semgrep-config.sh script that produces a validated .semgrep.yml with mandatory and stack-specific security rulesets
**Why**: This is the foundation for all Semgrep integration — the CI template and onboarding both depend on a reliable, stack-aware config generator
**Scope**:
- IN: generate-semgrep-config.sh script, detect-stack.sh consumption, .semgrep.yml output, semgrep --validate, mandatory rulesets (p/security-audit, p/owasp-top-ten, p/cwe-top-25), stack-specific ruleset mapping (python-poetry -> p/python, node-npm -> p/javascript, golang -> p/golang, convention-based/unknown -> mandatory only)
- OUT: Custom ruleset handling (consumed via dso-config.conf but not validated beyond path existence), air-gapped/vendored rules, Semgrep installation

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, running generate-semgrep-config.sh in a Python project produces a .semgrep.yml containing p/security-audit, p/owasp-top-ten, p/cwe-top-25, and p/python
  <- Satisfies: "A generate-semgrep-config.sh script produces a .semgrep.yml with mandatory rulesets plus stack-specific rulesets mapped from detect-stack.sh output"
- When this story is complete, running generate-semgrep-config.sh in a Node project produces a .semgrep.yml containing mandatory rulesets and p/javascript
  <- Satisfies: "A generate-semgrep-config.sh script produces a .semgrep.yml with mandatory rulesets plus stack-specific rulesets mapped from detect-stack.sh output"
- When this story is complete, the generated .semgrep.yml passes semgrep --validate without errors
  <- Satisfies: "The generated config passes semgrep --validate"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] generate-semgrep-config.sh produces YAML from shell — unit tests must verify output for each stack type
- [Reliability] Script must use REPO_ROOT (not relative paths) to work correctly in worktree contexts

comments.body: Done definition update: semgrep --validate checks YAML syntax and rule structure but does not fetch remote rulesets. A passing --validate confirms config is well-formed but does not guarantee registry availability at scan time. This is a known limitation — registry availability is a CI-time concern addressed by story 4c44-9805.
comments.author: Claude

comments.body: Consideration: [Interface Contract] generate-semgrep-config.sh writes .semgrep.yml to REPO_ROOT (git rev-parse --show-toplevel). Both dependent stories (4c44-9805 CI template and c9aa-abda onboarding) must reference this same path. The CI template's semgrep scan command should use --config .semgrep.yml (relative to repo root, which is the CI working directory).
comments.author: Claude
